title: High-Speed Visible Light Transceiver
overview: Designed and fabricated a real-time Visible Light Communication (VLC) transceiver capable of realtime HD Video transmission using high-speed LED modulation and photodiode-based signal reception. Developed an analog optical receiver front-end using a BPW34 photodiode and OPA380 transimpedance amplifier for MHz-range light signal detection and conditioning.
architecture: [Rpi4 Tx Baseband] ─(UART)─> [TC4420 MOSFET Driver] -> [IRLZ44N MOSFET] -> [LED] ─(Optical Light)─> [BPW34 Photodiode] ─> [OPA380 TIA Stage] ─> [LMV7219 Comparator] ─(UART)─> [Rpi4 Rx]
specifications: Texas Instruments OPA380 high-speed, transimpedance operational amplifier converting microamp photocurrent to voltage.
LMV7219 high-speed comparator squaring output wave logic levels to match Raspberry Pi 3.3V logic profiles.
Custom C++ baseband driver directly polling Raspberry Pi 4B registers for raw low-latency bitstreams.

title: Underground Cable Fault Detection System
overview: Developed a portable diagnostic hardware prototype utilizing signal pulse reflection principles (similar to Time-Domain Reflectometry) to precisely pinpoint short and open circuit faults in buried infrastructure. Programmed an embedded microcontroller to interpret voltage drops and calculate exact distance-to-fault metrics, significantly reducing the manual labor required for physical network troubleshooting.
architecture:
[Pulse Generator] ─(Signal Pulse)─> [Damaged Cable Under Test]
                                            │ (Reflection)
[Microcontroller ADC] ◄── [Signal Conditioning] ◄┘
specifications: Custom signal generator producing nanosecond pulses onto the transmission line.
High-speed analog capturing conditioning circuit with op-amps and clamping diodes.
Microcontroller timer capture registry to record transmission and arrival reflection times.
LCD indicator displaying calculated distance-to-fault and cable impedance changes.

title: IoT-Based Secure Access & Data Logging System
overview: Engineered a localized hardware access control prototype bridging serial sensor inputs with cloud-based monitoring. Integrated RFID authentication with a Wi-Fi-enabled microcontroller to execute real-time, encrypted database logging, ensuring secure, authorized-only facility access and traceable personnel telemetry.
architecture:
[RFID Reader RC522] ──(SPI)──> [ESP32 Wi-Fi Micro] ──(Secure HTTPS)──> [Database Logging Server]
                                                │ (GPIO)
                                                ▼
                                          [Solenoid Door Lock Relays]
specifications: MFRC522 13.56 MHz RFID reader module driving SPI serial interface.
ESP32 Wi-Fi enabled controller running secure web client sessions.
Optocoupler-isolated relay switching circuits driving electrical solenoid deadbolt locks.
Real-time clock integration for accurate time-stamping of local database entries.
